graph-vault-migration.title: Graphbuilding Fusion P1 — markdown-vault migration round-trip
graph-vault-migration.problem_statement: The claim graph lives only as a sqlite db in a scattered dotdir (~/.graphbuilding/graph.db): not hand-editable, not git-tracked, and reads force repair + full recompute because the write path never guaranteed read invariants.
graph-vault-migration.jtbd: When I want to trust and hand-fix my claim graph, I want it stored as structured markdown I can edit and version, so I can rebuild a queryable index from it losslessly and stop re-mining on every read.
graph-vault-migration.user_context: Single-tenant fork owner (saris). Phase 1 of the multi-phase graphbuilding-fusion epic (docs/graphbuilding-fusion-design.md). Deep native rebuild on this repo's stack; the ~/.claude/skills/graphbuilding scripts are a behavioral oracle, not a copy target.

scope_map.metric: round-trip fidelity (binary): 9/9 tables byte-equal AND 5/5 gap species exact

metric.name: round_trip_fidelity
metric.baseline: 6/7 prior tables matched; aliases 834 exported -> 779 imported (55 dangling-FK rows dropped); 5/5 species reproduced
metric.target: 9/9 tables byte-equal (aliases 834=834) AND 5/5 gap species exact (frontier 65, debate 0, replication 473, coverage 49, white-space 312)
metric.user_value_check: a green round-trip means wiki/graph/ markdown is the hand-editable, git-tracked source of truth and the sqlite index rebuilds with zero loss
metric.keep_revert_rule: keep only if pytest is green AND source-db md5 unchanged; revert otherwise
metric.simplicity_criterion: one shared root() helper, no inline COALESCE(canonical_id,id) anywhere

acceptance_criteria.text: 9 tables round-trip byte-equal (per-row diff): papers 98, sections 789, paper_authors 97, entities 1444, predicates 46, claims 1052, entity_edges 543, citation_links 0, aliases 834

graph-native-gaps.title: Native Gap Scanner — replace oracle gaps.py with repo-native analytics
graph-native-gaps.problem_statement: The 5-species gap scanner lives in the oracle dotdir (~/.claude/skills/graphbuilding/scripts/gaps.py), uses broken COALESCE(canonical_id,id) for entity resolution, and is not in this repo — it cannot evolve with the vault.
graph-native-gaps.jtbd: When I scan my claim graph for research gaps, I want a native gap scanner in this repo that uses root() (not broken COALESCE), runs on the derived db, and produces identical results to the oracle, so the analytics engine lives with the vault and evolves with it.
graph-native-gaps.user_context: Single-tenant fork owner (saris). Phase 2 of the graphbuilding-fusion epic.

scope_map.metric: gap count parity with oracle: 899 total, per-species exact (frontier 65, debate 0, replication 473, coverage 49, white-space 312)

metric.keep_revert_rule: keep only if all gap counts match oracle exactly; revert otherwise
metric.simplicity_criterion: one shared graph_db.root() for entity resolution, no oracle imports

graph-resolve-dedup.title: Entity Resolution + Embedding Dedup
graph-resolve-dedup.problem_statement: 689 variant entities exist in the graph. Same concepts under different names fragment gap counts and weaken the knowledge graph.
graph-resolve-dedup.jtbd: When I want to clean up duplicate entities, I need a tool that finds likely duplicates using exact matching and semantic similarity, ranks them by confidence, and presents them for human review.
graph-resolve-dedup.user_context: Single-tenant fork owner. Phase 3 of graphbuilding-fusion epic.

scope_map.metric: number of real duplicate entity pairs found (qualitative — human validates)

metric.name: duplicate_detection
metric.target: detect exact-name duplicates AND fuzzy semantic duplicates, ranked by confidence
metric.how_measured: uv run python scripts/graph-resolve.py --json | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(f'{len(d)} proposals')"
metric.keep_revert_rule: keep if P1+P2 suites stay green; revert otherwise

uv run python -m pytest tests/test_graph_resolve.py -q

graph-full-paper-retrieval.title: Full-Paper Retrieval for /graph
graph-full-paper-retrieval.problem_statement: Graph traversal can surface a claim or gap but cannot open the related paper's full text, so 'trace a claim/gap -> read the paper -> reason toward my next contribution' dead-ends at the section summaries already in papers/<slug>.md.
graph-full-paper-retrieval.jtbd: When I trace a claim or research gap, I want to open the related paper's full text and reason with full context (and a repeatable system so any paper I add or re-add later flows into that same full-context tracing), so I can derive next-paper proposals and proof-of-concept toy models grounded in real text.
graph-full-paper-retrieval.user_context: Single-tenant fork owner (saris). Phase 4 of the graphbuilding-fusion epic. macOS, uv-run Python 3.12, /graph skill v1.10. Today /graph gaps names entities/claims but the user must leave the tool and hunt the paper's text by hand across >=4 disk locations; papers/<slug>.md carries only summaries.

metric.name: in_graph_papers_full_text_retrievable
metric.baseline: 0 (no full text indexed; only summaries exist)
metric.target: all Tier-A papers (22 on current graph-export; resolver may rescue a few bare-dir entries that contain a single inner .md) imported + indexed; a seeded claim/query returns its source paper's passage in top-K with provenance; import+build idempotent

metric.name: in_graph_papers_full_text_retrievable
metric.baseline: 0 (no full text indexed; only section summaries exist in papers/<slug>.md)
metric.target: Tier-A papers (>=22 on current graph-export.json: 22 source_path values end in .md and all 22 files exist on disk; spec's '~38' was a stale pre-migration estimate) each get a byte-equal .full.md, the graph BM25 index builds over those bodies only, and a seeded claim/query returns its source paper's passage in top-K with provenance; import+build idempotent
metric.user_value_check: today gap/claim -> full paper text is impossible; after, it is one `/graph read`, and adding the next paper makes it instantly traceable
metric.how_measured: uv run python -m pytest tests/test_graph_fulltext.py -q  (AC1-AC6,AC9) AND make test-graph (AC8 no-regression)
metric.keep_revert_rule: keep only if tests/test_graph_fulltext.py is green AND make test-graph stays green AND a re-run diff of .full.md + index is empty (idempotent); revert otherwise
metric.simplicity_criterion: reuse contextual-prefix.pick_prefix_tier / bm25-index.query / rerank.rerank by importing + pointing CHUNKS_DIR/BM25_DIR at graph paths; do NOT fork or edit those modules and do NOT touch graph-build.py

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Resolver + import writes one .full.md per Tier-A paper (>=22), each a byte-equal copy of its source .md body with the type:paper-fulltext / slug / arxiv_id / source_path / paper frontmatter contract.

evidence: 95 .full.md written (>=22 target); real frontmatter verified type:paper-fulltext/slug/arxiv_id/source_path/paper wikilink; body byte-equal. pytest -k 'import' 8 passed

Planner -k strings must match real test names. Evaluator must read exit codes, not trust 'no tests ran' as pass.

graph-bridge.title: Proposal Finder — graph-bridge.py (ranked, grounded white-space bridge proposals)
graph-bridge.problem_statement: The graph can name white-space gaps (community pairs with zero claim-edges) but cannot turn them into ranked, justified next-paper proposals, so the analysis stops short of the research output it exists to serve.
graph-bridge.jtbd: When I see white-space in my claim graph, I want a ranked shortlist of justified bridge proposals grounded in the real papers, so I can pick my next paper's contribution instead of eyeballing 116 gaps.
graph-bridge.user_context: saris (single-tenant fork owner), asleep; built autonomously under an explicit 'skip grill + keep stress testing until it works' directive. macOS, uv Python 3.12, /graph skill, derived db at .vault-meta/graph/graph.db. Awaiting human ratification on wake — nothing pushed or merged.

metric.name: gold-anchor surfaces in top-N AND 100-case stress test well-formed+grounded+deterministic
metric.target: gold anchor (VTON-community <-> diffusion-sampling/distillation-community) in top-N; 100-case stress >= 95% well-formed+grounded+non-degenerate+no-crash; deterministic seed=42

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Ranks white-space gaps into proposals with a per-proposal signal breakdown; deterministic (seed=42), repeat runs identical.
acceptance_criteria.verification: uv run python -m pytest tests/test_graph_bridge.py -k rank_deterministic -q 2>&1 | grep -qE '[1-9][0-9]* passed' && ! ( uv run python -m pytest tests/test_graph_bridge.py -k rank_deterministic -q 2>&1 | grep -qE 'failed|error' )

validation.business_metric: met: gold anchor (VTON<->diffusion/distillation) ranks #1 on live db (score 0.7057, dr 1.0); 100-case stress 100% well-formed+grounded+non-degenerate; deterministic ranking (md5 identical x3); FR7 flags 8 already_proposed without dropping. technical: graph-bridge reconstructs the seed=42 Louvain clustering identically to graph-gaps (canonical nodes via graph_db.root, weighted claim edges) rather than recomputing differently — verified the live gold anchor ranks #1, which only holds if the substrate matches.

Ranking determinism (BR2) is byte-identical across runs; --synthesize output is NOT deterministic because it shells out to live `claude -p` (claude-cli tier) — that is intended opt-in egress per FR5, not an AC, so non-identical synthesize md5 is correct behavior.
product: FR7 flags-not-drops: 8 of top-100 carry already_proposed=true (endpoints touch a hand-written bridge entity) and remain ranked — the user still sees them, which is the point.
process: Evaluator proved zero-egress on the default path empirically by placing a failing `claude` shim first on PATH and confirming it never fired — stronger evidence than reading the args.synthesize guard. Code-reading alone would have missed that `claude` IS present in this env (so the egress tier is reachable) yet correctly gated.

graph-semantic-bridge.title: Semantic Bridge — Claude-Code Directions Report from the Graph
graph-semantic-bridge.problem_statement: The heuristic graph-bridge.py ranks WHERE the white-space bridges are, but cannot reason about WHETHER they are any good, so there is no proposals.md-grade research-directions analysis generated from the graph.
graph-semantic-bridge.jtbd: When I see ranked white-space bridges in my claim graph, I want Claude Code to read the grounded evidence plus my research situation and write a proposals.md-grade directions report (decision matrix, per-direction harsh takedown, execution probe), with every cited paper/entity verified against the real graph, so I can choose my next paper instead of hand-writing the analysis myself.
graph-semantic-bridge.user_context: Primary user: saris (single-tenant fork owner). Goal: a first-author top-venue paper strong enough to be a top-tier PhD ticket. VTON + applied diffusion strength; theory muscle building; ~4h/week. macOS, uv Python 3.12, /graph skill v1.10. `claude` IS on PATH (/Users/saris.kia.adm/.local/bin/claude); no API key in repo .env. Phase 6 of the graphbuilding-fusion epic. Today /graph bridge returns ranked pairs + a thin --synthesize line; a proposals.md-grade document is hand-written.

metric.name: grounded_structurally_complete_directions_report
metric.baseline: 0 (no semantic report generator; only the thin heuristic --synthesize line)
metric.target: 1 grounded structurally-complete report saved + grounding gate proven (clean-accept, dirty-retry, cap-reject) by tests with a faked engine

metric.name: grounded_structurally_complete_directions_report
metric.baseline: 0 (no semantic report generator; only the thin heuristic graph-bridge --synthesize line)

metric.user_value_check: today the analysis is hand-written; after, it is one AFK command that returns a harsh, grounded directions doc — and the user can trust the citations because the gate rejects hallucinations
metric.how_measured: uv run python -m pytest tests/test_graph_propose.py -q (AC1-AC7,AC10 offline, faked --claude-cmd) AND make test-graph (44/4) && make test-fulltext (45) && make test-bridge (26) (AC8 no-regression) AND one live `uv run python scripts/graph-propose.py` run with grep on the saved report (AC9 evidence-only)
metric.keep_revert_rule: keep only if tests/test_graph_propose.py is green AND make test-graph (44/4) + make test-fulltext (45) + make test-bridge (26) stay green AND graph-build.py byte-unchanged AND ~/Desktop/research/proposals.md byte-unchanged; revert otherwise
metric.simplicity_criterion: import graph-bridge.build_proposals for candidates and subprocess graph-retrieve.py for passages (as graph-bridge already does) — do NOT fork either, do NOT touch graph-build.py or the 9-table round-trip; the only new I/O is the claude -p subprocess + the timestamped report write

learning.evaluator_2026-06-07: harness/product: a grounding gate whose extractor only surfaces ALL-CAPS + backtick tokens cannot reject a prose-case hallucination ('the Garment Diffusion Transfer paper'); adversarial fake engine produced a clean exit-0 '0/0 verified ✓' report with 3 fabricated cites. Tests were self-confirming (only fed ALL-CAPS FABRICATED-*). Fix: compel all cites into a checked form in the prompt AND assert prose/author-year hallucination is rejected in a test, OR broaden the verifier; also guard the 0/0 vacuous-pass.
